title: Weekly Surge In Google Trends For Sudden Appliance Breakdowns Signals Discretionary Sector Rebound
thesis: A sharp weekly increase in searches related to emergency appliance repairs often precedes increased consumer spending on home services and replacements. Discretionary retailers and home improvement benefit as consumers rush to replace or fix broken appliances.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for appliance repair keywords
signal_logic: Enter long XLY when weekly Google Trends for appliance breakdown-related terms rises over 20% from the prior week
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLY when weekly Google Trends for appliance breakdown-related terms rises over 20% from the prior week Exit: Exit after 4 weeks or when search volume returns to baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for appliance repair keywords via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly Google Trends data for appliance repair spikes frequently during seasonal appliance wear periods.

schema.idea: Weekly surge in Google Trends for sudden appliance breakdowns signals discretionary sector rebound

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for appliance repair keywords

schema.behavior: A sharp weekly increase in searches related to emergency appliance repairs often precedes increased consumer spending on home services and replacements.

market_impact.rationale: Discretionary retailers and home improvement benefit as consumers rush to replace or fix broken appliances.

trade_logic.entry: Enter long XLY when weekly Google Trends for appliance breakdown-related terms rises over 20% from the prior week
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 4 weeks or when search volume returns to baseline

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly Google Trends data for appliance repair spikes frequently during seasonal appliance wear periods.

title: Weekly Jump In Google Trends For Job Quitting Searches Signals Labor Market Tightness Benefiting Financial Sector
thesis: Increased interest in quitting jobs indicates labor market tightness and wage inflation expectations, often followed by increased borrowing and financial activity. Banks and financial companies benefit from higher loan demand and rising interest rate environments tied to strong labor markets.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for job quitting and resignations
signal_logic: Enter long XLF when weekly Google Trends for job quitting rises 15%+ over previous week
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLF when weekly Google Trends for job quitting rises 15%+ over previous week Exit: Exit after 6 weeks or when search interest declines below 5% above baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for job quitting and resignations via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor market sentiment shifts reflected in searches happen frequently, especially near seasonal cycles.

title: Usgs Earthquake Frequency Surge Signals Short-term Disruption Recovery Bounce In Industrials
thesis: A sudden increase in moderate earthquake occurrences correlates with temporary supply chain and infrastructure disruptions followed by rebound construction and industrial activity. Industrial companies often benefit from increased repair and rebuilding efforts following seismic events.
signal_logic: Enter long XLI when daily count of magnitude 4+ earthquakes in US rises 50% above 14-day average
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLI when daily count of magnitude 4+ earthquakes in US rises 50% above 14-day average Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or when earthquake counts revert to normal
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake daily activity counts via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Earthquake swarms and clusters happen multiple times annually, triggering local industrial activity bursts.
